data.name: Users Identified on Specific Host in Last Month
data.description: Lists all the distinct users that were identified on a specific host in the last month
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d case_sensitive = false |
preset = authentication_story |
filter auth_client  = $host and auth_identity != null |
dedup auth_identity |
fields auth_identity , auth_client , action_local_ip , action_remote_ip

data.name: CVEs Related to a Specific Host
data.description: Lists CVEs related to a specific host
data.query_text:
dataset = va_cves |
arrayexpand affected_hosts |
filter affected_hosts contains $host |
sort desc severity_score

data.name: Unusual Processes Executed on Specific Host in Last Month
data.description: Lists unusual processes that were executed on a specific host in the last month
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d case_sensitive = false |
dataset = xdr_data |
filter agent_hostname = $host |
fields action_process_image_sha256 as sha256sig, action_process_image_path as path, event_id, agent_hostname as hostname  |
comp count(event_id) as counter by sha256sig, path,hostname |
filter counter < 3 |
sort desc counter

data.name: Removable Devices Connected to the Specific Host in Last Month
data.description: Lists any removable devices connected to a specific host in the last month
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d case_sensitive = false | dataset = xdr_data | filter event_type = ENUM.MOUNT and event_sub_type = ENUM.MOUNT_DRIVE_MOUNT and agent_hostname = $host
| alter Drive_Type = json_extract(to_json_string(action_mount_device_info),"$.storage_device_drive_type"), Filesystem = json_extract_scalar(to_json_string(action_mount_device_info),"$.storage_device_filesystem"), Drive_Letter = json_extract_scalar(to_json_string(action_mount_device_info),"$.storage_device_mount_point"), Device_Serial_Number = json_extract_scalar(to_json_string(action_mount_device_info),"$.storage_device_serial_number")
| filter Drive_Type = "2" //2 is a removable device
| fields agent_hostname, Drive_Letter, Drive_Type, Filesystem, Device_Serial_Number, action_device_usb_vendor_name, action_device_usb_product_name

data.name: Machines Specific User Logged in from in Last Month
data.description: Filters by event ID 4624 and then aggregates all the information related to the hostname and IP addresses for a specified user from the last month
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d |
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = EVENT_LOG | filter action_evtlog_event_id = 4624
| alter user_name = arrayindex(regextract(action_evtlog_message,"Account\sName\:\t\t(\S+)"),1)
| filter user_name contains $user
| alter logon_process = arrayindex(regextract(action_evtlog_message, "Logon\sProcess:\t\t(\S+)"),0)
| alter logon_type = arrayindex(regextract(action_evtlog_message, "Logon\sType:\t\t(\d+)"),0)
| alter security_id = arrayindex(regextract(action_evtlog_message, "Security\sID:\t\t(\S+)"),0)
| alter account_name = arrayindex(regextract(action_evtlog_message, "Account\sName:\t\t(\S+)"),0)
| alter account_domain = arrayindex(regextract(action_evtlog_message, "Account\sDomain\:\t\t(\S+)"),0)
| alter network_address = arrayindex(regextract(action_evtlog_message, "Network\sAddress:\t(\S+)") ,0)
| alter workstation_name = arrayindex(regextract(action_evtlog_message, "Workstation\sName\:\t(\S+)"),0)
| alter host_name = coalesce(account_name,workstation_name )
| filter (host_name != "-" and network_address != "-")
| filter host_name != null
| comp count_distinct(host_name), values(host_name) as hosts, values(network_address) as ip by user_name

data.name: Geolocations Specific User Logged in From in Last Month
data.description: Lists the geolocations that a specific user logged in from in the last month
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| preset = authentication_story
| filter auth_identity contains $user and action_country != "-"
| comp values(action_country) as action_country by auth_client, auth_identity , auth_identity_display_name , auth_service
| arrayexpand action_country

data.name: Unusual Processes That Were Executed By A Specifiec User In Last Month
data.description: Lists all the proccess that were initiated by a given username that weren't conducted in the last month, but were performed in the last 24 hours
data.query_text:
config timeframe between "30d" and "2d"
| preset = xdr_process
| dedup agent_hostname,actor_effective_username, action_process_image_path , action_process_image_name  , action_process_image_sha256
| join type = right conflict_strategy = right (config timeframe =1d|preset = xdr_process|dedup agent_hostname,actor_effective_username, action_process_image_path , action_process_image_name  , action_process_image_sha256|alter executable = action_process_image_name|fields -action_process_image_name) as right right.executable = action_process_image_name
| filter action_process_image_name = null and (action_process_username contains $username or actor_effective_username contains $username)
| alter action_process_image_name = executable

data.name: PANW NGFW | Files Specific Users Downloaded in Last Month
data.description: Lists files that were downloaded by the user in the last month by searching the URL events and looking for a URL that ends with a file exstention. This indicates that this is a download file operation.
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_url_raw
| filter uri ~= "[\w\.\/]+\/\w+\.\w+$"
| fields source_ip , url_domain , uri ,dest_ip ,action ,users, source_user ,app ,app_category , dest_port , file_url , rule_matched , dest_location
| comp values(source_ip ) as source_ip_address, values(uri) as request_uri , values(app ) as application_name, values(app_category) as application_catgeory, count() as total_download,count_distinct(uri) as different_uri by users
| filter users contains $user

data.name: Usual Ratio of Failed vs Successful Logins for Specific Users in Last Month
data.description: Lists the daily average number of authentication events performed in the last month by a specific user
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| preset = authentication_story
| filter auth_identity contains $user
| bin _time span = 24h
| alter success_boolean = if(auth_outcome = "SUCCESS", 1 , 0)
| alter failure_boolean = if(auth_outcome = "FAILURE", 1 , 0)
| filter auth_outcome != null and auth_identity != null
| comp count() as total_authentication_events, sum(success_boolean ) as success_count, values(auth_outcome_reason ) as auth_outcome_reason, values(associated_products ) as products , values(action_country ) as source_country, values(auth_outcome) as outcome, sum(failure_boolean) as failure_count  by  auth_identity  , auth_client , auth_target
| alter success_ratio = divide(success_count , total_authentication_events )
| sort asc success_ratio
| fields auth_identity, success_ratio , *

data.name: Active Directory Groups of Users
data.description: Lists all the Active Direcory groups a user is a member of
data.query_text:
dataset = pan_dss_raw
| filter netbios_and_sam_account_name contains $user or upn contains $user or sam_account_name contains $user
| fields  display_name , security_groups , sam_account_name , upn ,netbios_and_sam_account_name , last_logon_timestamp , guid , dn,ou
| arrayexpand security_groups

data.name: Changes Made to User Permission Levels in Last Month (Active Directory domain)
data.description: Lists the user's permission level changes in Active Directory in the last month using event ID 4728. Note: When Active Directory objects, such as a user, group, computer, is added to a security global group, event ID 4728 gets logged. Changes in the user's security group will affect the user's permission levels.
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d | dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = EVENT_LOG
| filter action_evtlog_event_id = 4728
| alter editor_sid = action_evtlog_data_fields -> SubjectUserSid
| alter editor_username = action_evtlog_data_fields -> SubjectUserName
| alter editor_domain = action_evtlog_data_fields -> SubjectDomainName
| alter editor_logonid = action_evtlog_data_fields -> SubjectLogonId
| alter target_group = action_evtlog_data_fields -> TargetUserName
| alter target_domain = action_evtlog_data_fields -> TargetDomainName
| alter target_member = action_evtlog_data_fields -> MemberName
| filter target_member contains $username or editor_username contains $username

data.name: Changes Made to User Permission Levels in Last Month on the Cloud
data.description: Lists Azure AD and AWS role management events in the last month, where the user's permissions are changed
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = msft_azure_ad_audit_raw
| filter category = "RoleManagement" and activityDisplayName = "Add member to role" and result in ("failure", "success")
| alter user = initiatedBy -> user.userPrincipalName, app = initiatedBy -> app.displayName
| alter source_user= coalesce(user,app), role_name= json_extract_scalar(targetResources , "$.0.modifiedProperties.1.newValue") , target_user= json_extract_scalar(targetResources , "$.0.userPrincipalName")
| filter target_user = $user
| filter app in (null,"Microsoft Office 365 Portal") or (app="MS-PIM")
| fields source_user, role_name,target_user, operationType ,activityDisplayName ,category , result , *
| union (dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName ~= "Attach\w+Policy$"
| alter roleName = json_extract_scalar(requestParameters , "$.policyArn") | filter requestParameters -> userName = $user)

data.name: Hosts Connected to Specific IP Address/Source in Last Month
data.description: Lists all the hosts that communicated with an IP address across all the normalized data sources in the last month
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d | preset = network_story
| filter action_local_ip = $ip or action_remote_ip = $ip or actor_remote_ip = $ip or agent_ip_addresses contains $ip
| comp count() as connections, values(action_local_ip ) as source , values(action_remote_ip) as `target` by action_local_ip ,action_remote_ip
| sort desc connections
| fields source , `target` , connections

data.name: Users Connected to Specific IP/Source in Last Month
data.description: Lists all the events with a specific IP address in the last month, and includes the applicable user fields
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d | preset = network_story
| filter action_remote_ip = $ip
| dedup actor_effective_username
| fields actor_effective_username ,action_local_ip , action_remote_ip , action_remote_port , action_app_id_transitions

data.name: Analyze Behavior Associated with Specific IP Address in Last Month (Types of traffic/ Protocols used)
data.description: Details the communication with a specific IP address (FW rules, protocols, FW zones, FW actions) in the last month, and includes the applicable data related to the IP address
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d | dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| fields source_ip ,dest_ip , app,app_category , users , source_user , action ,sub_type, protocol  , rule_matched ,to_zone , risk_of_app ,url_category //,session_end_reason
| filter source_ip = $ip or dest_ip = $ip
| filter (risk_of_app not contains "Informational")
| comp values(app) as app,values(app_category) as app_category,values(source_user) as source_user,values(action) as action,values(protocol) as protocol,values(rule_matched) as rule_matched,values(to_zone) as to_zone,values(risk_of_app) as risk_of_app,values(url_category) as url_category,values(sub_type) as sub_type by source_ip
| fields source_ip ,protocol ,app, app_category ,url_category , action, *
| filter source_user != null

data.name: Machines Connected with a Specific Domain in Last Month
data.description: Searches the given domain across all the normalized data sources over the last month, and lists the related machines that were communicating with this domain
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d | dataset = xdr_data
| fields action_local_ip, action_remote_ip, agent_hostname , action_external_hostname
| filter action_external_hostname contains $domain
| comp values(agent_hostname) as local_hosts, values(action_local_ip) as local_ips, values(action_remote_ip) as remote_ips by action_external_hostname
| fields local_hosts , local_ips , remote_ips , action_external_hostname

data.name: Users Connected with Specific Domain in Last Month
data.description: Searches the given domain across all the normalized data sources over the last month, and lists the related user details
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d | dataset = xdr_data
| fields actor_effective_username , action_remote_ip , action_external_hostname
| filter action_external_hostname contains $domain
| filter actor_effective_username != null
| dedup actor_effective_username
| fields actor_effective_username , action_remote_ip , action_external_hostname

data.name: Prevalence of Specific Domain "communication with" in this Organization in Last Month
data.description: Details statistics on the communication with a given domain in the last month
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = xdr_data
| filter action_external_hostname contains $domain
| fields dst_action_external_hostname , action_external_hostname
| filter action_external_hostname != null
| bin _time span = 1d
| comp count() as c by action_external_hostname , _time
| comp avg(c) as average_per_day,list(c) as count_values, var(c) as variance, earliest(_time) as `earliest`, latest(_time) as `latest`,count_distinct(_time) as days_appeared_count  by action_external_hostname addrawdata = true
| alter stdev = pow(variance ,0.5)

data.name: Emails Sent / Received from a Specific Domain in Last 3 Months
data.description: After reviewing the emails from Office365 and Gmail, lists the emails sent/received from a given domain in the last 3 months, and includes the applicable fields
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 3mo | dataset = email_data
| filter to_string(from) contains $domain or to_string(from_normalized) contains $domain or to_string(sender) contains $domain or to_string(sender_normalized) contains $domain

data.name: Query File Activities Related to Specific Hash in Last Month
data.description: List the file actions that are related to a specific hash in last month (Create/Write/Rename)
data.query_text:
config case_sensitive = false timeframe = 30d | dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and (event_sub_type = ENUM.FILE_WRITE or event_sub_type = ENUM.FILE_RENAME or event_sub_type = ENUM.FILE_CREATE_NEW or event_sub_type = ENUM.FILE_REMOVE )
| filter action_file_sha256 = $hash or action_file_md5 = $hash
| fields agent_hostname,agent_ip_addresses, actor_effective_username, actor_process_image_name ,event_sub_type ,action_file_path
| join conflict_strategy = left type = left (config case_sensitive = false timeframe = 1Y | dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and event_sub_type = ENUM.FILE_REMOVE
| filter action_file_sha256 = $hash or action_file_md5 = $hash |alter deleted = "yes" | fields agent_hostname , action_file_path , deleted ) as delete_check delete_check.agent_hostname = agent_hostname and delete_check.action_file_path = action_file_path
| alter deleted = if(deleted = null, "no" , deleted)
| fields agent_hostname,agent_ip_addresses, actor_effective_username, actor_process_image_name ,event_sub_type ,action_file_path, deleted

data.name: Process Executions of Specific Hash in Last Month
data.description: Searches for the given hash, and lists all the events in the last month where this hash was executed and observed by the XDR agent. This can help identify the endpoints which have executed a suspicous file/malware
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d | dataset = xdr_data
| filter action_process_image_sha256 = $hash or action_process_image_md5 = $hash or action_file_sha256 = $hash or action_module_sha256 = $hash
| fields _time, agent_hostname,event_type, event_sub_type, actor_effective_username , action_process_image_path, action_process_image_sha256, action_process_image_md5, actor_process_image_name , action_file_path
| view column order = populated

data.name: PANW NGFW |  Allowed vs. Denied Traffic Distribution
data.description: Calculates the distribution of Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) allowed vs. denied traffic in the last 7 days by analyzing firewall actions
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp count() as action_count by action
| sort desc action_count

data.name: PANW NGFW |  Top 10 Applications by Network Traffic
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) applications generating the most network traffic (data volume) over the last 7 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp sum(bytes_total) as total_bytes by app
| sort desc total_bytes
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Source IP Addresses by Session Count
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) source IP addresses with the highest number of network sessions
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp count() as session_count by source_ip
| sort desc session_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Destination Zones by Blocked Traffic
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) destination zones that experienced the most blocked traffic over the last 30 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter action in ("deny","block")
| comp count() as blocked_count by to_zone
| sort desc blocked_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Protocols by Frequency
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) used protocols in the network traffic over the last 7 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp count() as protocol_count by protocol
| sort desc protocol_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Users by Data Transfer
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) users responsible for the highest volume of data transfer in the last 30 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp sum(bytes_total) as total_bytes by source_user
| sort desc total_bytes
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Average Session Duration per Application
data.description: Calculates the average session duration for each Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) application over the last 7 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp avg(total_time_elapsed) as avg_duration by app
| sort desc avg_duration

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Destination Ports by Bytes Sent
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) destination ports that received the most data in the last 7 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp sum(bytes_sent) as total_bytes_sent by dest_port
| sort desc total_bytes_sent
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Allowed vs. Denied Traffic Distribution
data.description: Calculates the Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) distribution of allowed vs. denied traffic within the last 7 days, providing insight into the balance between traffic allowed through the firewall versus the traffic denied
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp count() as action_count by action
| sort desc action_count

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Destination IPs by Data Volume
data.description: Identifies the top 10 Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW)  destination IPs that received the most data in the last 30 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp sum(bytes_received) as total_bytes_received by dest_ip
| sort desc total_bytes_received
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Source-Destination Pairs by Session Count
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) source-destination IP pairs with the highest number of network sessions in the last 7 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp count() as session_count by source_ip, dest_ip
| sort desc session_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Average Bytes per Session by Source Zone
data.description: Calculates the average number of Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) bytes transferred per session for each source zone over the last 30 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp avg(bytes_total) as avg_bytes_per_session by from_zone
| sort desc avg_bytes_per_session

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Rules Triggered by Traffic
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW)  firewall rules that were triggered most frequently in the last 7 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp count() as rule_trigger_count by rule_matched
| sort desc rule_trigger_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Source Locations by Data Volume
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) source locations that generated the most traffic in terms of bytes transferred in the last 30 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp sum(bytes_total) as total_bytes by source_location
| sort desc total_bytes
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Packets Sent by Application
data.description: Lists the total number of Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) packets sent by each application over the last 7 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp sum(packets_sent) as total_packets_sent by app
| sort desc total_packets_sent

data.name: PANW NGFW | Average Packets per Session by Source User
data.description: Calculates the average number of Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) packets per session for each source user in the last 30 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp avg(packets_total) as avg_packets_per_session by source_user
| sort desc avg_packets_per_session

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Specific Applications by Data Volume
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) applications generating the most network traffic (data volume) over the last 7 days, filtering by specific applications
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter app in ($application)
| comp sum(bytes_total) as total_bytes by app
| sort desc total_bytes
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Specific Destination Zones by Blocked Traffic
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) destination zones that experienced the most blocked traffic over the last 30 days, filtering by specific destination zones
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter action = "deny" and to_zone in ($to_zone)
| comp count() as blocked_count by to_zone
| sort desc blocked_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Users by Bytes Transferred
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) users responsible for the highest volume of data transfer in the last 30 days, filtering by specific users
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter source_user in ($source_user)
| comp sum(bytes_total) as total_bytes by source_user
| sort desc total_bytes
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Source-Destination Pairs by Session Count
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) source-destination IP pairs with the highest number of network sessions in the last 7 days, filtering by specific source and destination IPs
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter source_ip in ($source_ip) and dest_ip in ($dest_ip)
| comp count() as session_count by source_ip, dest_ip
| sort desc session_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Threat Categories Detected
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) threat categories detected by the firewall to help determine the most frequent types of threats faced by the network
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| comp count() as threat_count by threat_category
| sort desc threat_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Threat-Generating Source IPs
data.description: Reveals the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) source IP addresses that generated the most threat logs, helping to identify malicious or compromised hosts in the network
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| comp count() as threat_count by source_ip
| sort desc threat_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Threat-Generating Destination IPs
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) destination IP addresses that have received the most traffic associated with threats to identify potential target systems
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| comp count() as threat_count by dest_ip
| sort desc threat_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Applications Associated with Threats
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) applications that are most frequently associated with detected threats
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| comp count() as threat_count by app
| sort desc threat_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Most Common Threat Actions
data.description: Lists the most common actions taken by the Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) in response to detected threats, such as allowing, blocking, or alerting on traffic
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| comp count() as action_count by action
| sort desc action_count

data.name: PANW NGFW | Threats by Source and Destination Zones
data.description: Calculates which Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) network zones most frequently involve the detection of threats
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| comp count() as threat_count by from_zone, to_zone
| sort desc threat_count

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Threat IDs Detected
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) threat IDs detected on the network
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| comp count() as threat_count by threat_id
| sort desc threat_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Files Involved in Threats
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) files detected involved in threats
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| comp count() as file_count by file_name
| sort desc file_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Threats by Direction of Attack
data.description: Categorizes the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) threats based on their direction of attack
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| comp count() as threat_count by direction_of_attack
| sort desc threat_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Source Users Involved in Threats
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW)  source users associated with detected threats
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| comp count() as threat_count by source_user
| sort desc threat_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Threats by Vendor Severity and Action
data.description: Categorizes the top Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) ten threats based on their severity and the actions taken by the firewall
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| comp count() as threat_count by vendor_severity, action
| sort desc threat_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Threats Targeting Specific Users
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) threats that target specific users by filtering the logs for user-based threats
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| filter source_user in ($source_user)
| comp count() as threat_count by source_user
| sort desc threat_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | Analysis of Threat Categories in Specific Zones
data.description: Categorizes Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) threats based on the source and destination zones
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| comp count() as threat_count by from_zone, to_zone, threat_category
| sort desc threat_count

data.name: PANW NGFW | Session-Based Threat Analysis for Specific Applications
data.description: Analyzes Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) threat logs based on specific applications and correlates threats to their sessions
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| filter app in ($application)
| comp count() as threat_count by session_id, app
| sort desc threat_count

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top Threats by Spyware Category
data.description: Identifies the most frequently detected Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) threats categorized as spyware
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| filter threat_category = "spyware"
| comp count() as spyware_count by threat_id, source_ip, dest_ip
| sort desc spyware_count

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top Denial of Service (DoS) Attacks Detected
data.description: Identifies the most common Denial of Service (DoS) attacks detected by the Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW)
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
| filter threat_category = "dos"
| comp count() as dos_attack_count by threat_id, source_ip, dest_ip
| sort desc dos_attack_count

data.name: PANW NGFW | Top 10 Destination Users by Session Count
data.description: Identifies the top ten Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) destination users based on the number of network sessions over the past 30 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| comp count() as session_count by dest_user
| sort desc session_count
| limit 10

data.name: PANW NGFW | High-Severity Spyware Threats Excluding DNS
data.description: Lists high-severity spyware threats detected in the network and filters out DNS-related traffic
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
|filter severity != "Informational" and severity != "Low" and severity != "Medium" and severity != null
|filter app not contains "dns"
|filter sub_type = "spyware"
| fields source_ip , source_port , dest_ip , dest_port , action, app, app_category , threat_category , threat_name , direction_of_attack , from_zone , to_zone ,*

data.name: PANW NGFW |  Hacking Tool Traffic Detection
data.description: Identifies Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) traffic associated with hacking tools in the network, helping analysts focus on potentially harmful activities
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
|filter threat_category contains  "hacktool"
| fields source_ip , source_port , dest_ip , dest_port , action, app, app_category , threat_category , threat_name , direction_of_attack , from_zone , to_zone ,*

data.name: PANW NGFW | Malware Download Detection Via PAN-OS NGFW on Endpoints
data.description: Identifies Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) malware downloads, such as viruses and wildfire-virus detections, occurring on endpoints
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_threat_raw
|filter sub_type ~= ".*wildfire-virus.*|.*virus.*|.*wildfire.*|.*virus.*"
| fields source_ip , source_port , dest_ip , dest_port , action, app, app_category , threat_category , threat_name , direction_of_attack , from_zone , to_zone ,*

data.name: PANW NGFW | Cryptocurrency Mining Traffic Detection
data.description: Detects Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) traffic associated with cryptocurrency mining applications, such as bitcoin and other miners
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
|filter app in ("bitcoin", "coinhave", "coinblind", "coinimp", "coinnebula", "crypto-loot", "deepminer", "jsecoin", "litecoin", "mineralt", "webmine", "cryptonoter", "coinhive")
| fields app, to_zone, dest_device_host ,dest_ip, dest_port, log_source_name, action, rule_matched, session_id, from_zone, source_ip, source_port, source_user_info_name, *

data.name: PANW NGFW | Detection of Malicious URL Categories
data.description: Identifies URL categories related to malicious content, such as malware, ransomware, and spyware in the Next-Generation-Firewall (NGFW) events
data.query_text:
dataset = panw_ngfw_url_raw
|filter url_category ~= ".*malware.*|.*ransomware.*|.*adware.*|.*command-and-control.*|.*hijack.*|spyware|.*virus.*|c2" or url_category_list ~= ".*malware.*|.*ransomware.*|.*adware.*|.*command-and-control.*|.*hijack.*|spyware|.*virus.*"
| fields source_ip , source_port , dest_ip , dest_port , action, app, app_category , from_zone , to_zone, user_agent ,*

data.name: All Emails Sent/Recieved by Given Email Address
data.description: Lists all the related emails that were sent/recieved by this email address
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| filter to_string(sender) contains $sender or to_string(recipients) contains $sender or to_string(cc_recipients) contains $sender or to_string(bcc_recipients) contains $sender
| fields _time, sender , sender_ip , recipients , from, attachments , has_attachments , *

data.name: Email Domains Communicated with via Email
data.description: Lists all the email domains that have been commuincated with over email
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| arrayexpand recipients
| alter recipient_address= recipients->address , sender_address = sender -> address
| dedup recipient_address, sender_address
| alter sender_domain = arrayindex(regextract(sender_address,".*\@(.*)"),0), recipient_domain = arrayindex(regextract(recipient_address,".*\@(.*)"),0 )
| comp values(sender_domain ) as sent_from, values(recipient_domain) as recieved_from

data.name: Office 365 | Emails Associated with Alerts
data.description: Lists the mail events that triggered a Microsoft Defender for Office 365 alert.
data.query_text:
dataset = msft_graph_security_alerts_raw
| filter detectionSource = "microsoftDefenderForOffice365"
| alter analyzedMessageEvidence = arrayindex(arrayfilter(evidence ->[] , "@element" -> ["@odata.type"] = "#microsoft.graph.security.analyzedMessageEvidence"), 0)
| alter internetMessageId = analyzedMessageEvidence -> internetMessageId
| join (dataset = email_data ) as emails emails.internet_message_id contains internetMessageId

data.name: Verify Email Sent by $sender Read
data.description: Checks whether the email that was recieved by $sender was opened by any of the recipients
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| alter country = sender_ip_location -> country,sender_address = sender -> address , is_read = o365_data -> is_read
| filter sender_address contains $sender
| fields  sender , is_read ,recipients ,*

data.name: Outbound Emails Sent to Private/Personal Email Account
data.description: Lists the people who have forwarded emails to their personal email account, which can indicate that somone is trying to leak sensitive data
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| alter sender_address = sender -> address
| alter recipients_address = arraymap(recipients , json_extract_scalar("@element","$.address"))
| arrayexpand recipients_address
| filter recipients_address ~= ".*gmail|yahoo|outlook|microsoft.*"
| fields sender , sender_address , recipients , recipients_address , attachments

data.name: Incoming Emails where Reply To is Different from Sender
data.description: Lists any incoming emails that replying to them would actually go to different email address
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| alter sender_address = sender -> address
| filter reply_to != null
| arrayexpand reply_to
| filter reply_to -> address != sender_address
| fields reply_to , sender , *

data.name: Emails with Attachments
data.description: Lists emails including attachments
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| filter has_attachments = true
| fields attachments , has_attachments , sender , recipients , from , *

data.name: Display Name for Given Email Address ($email_address)
data.description: Details the display name of a given $email_address
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| arrayexpand recipients
| alter recipient_address= recipients->address ,recipient_name = recipients -> name, sender_address = sender -> address ,sender_name = sender -> name
| filter recipient_address contains $email_address or sender_address contains $email_address
| dedup recipient_address, sender_address
| fields recipient_address,recipient_name,sender_address,sender_name,*

data.name: Email Address for Given Display Name ($display_name)
data.description: Details the $email_address of a given $display_name
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| arrayexpand recipients
| alter recipient_address= recipients->address ,recipient_name = recipients -> name, sender_address = sender -> address ,sender_name = sender -> name
| filter recipient_name contains $display_name or sender_name contains $display_name
| dedup recipient_address, sender_address
| fields recipient_address,recipient_name,sender_address,sender_name,*

data.name: Outbound Emails Sent to External Mailboxes
data.description: Lists the emails that left the organization and headed to external mailboxes
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| arrayexpand recipients
| alter recipient_address= recipients->address , sender_address = sender -> address
| alter sender_domain = arrayindex(regextract(sender_address,".*\@(.*)"),0), recipient_domain = arrayindex(regextract(recipient_address,".*\@(.*)"),0 )
| filter sender_domain = $company_domain and recipient_domain != $company_domain
| fields _time , sender_domain , recipient_domain , sender , recipients ,return_path  , *

data.name: Inbound Emails Received from External Mailboxes
data.description: Lists emails that were recieved from external mailboxes to an email address associated with the organization
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| arrayexpand recipients
| alter recipient_address= recipients->address , sender_address = sender -> address
| alter sender_domain = arrayindex(regextract(sender_address,".*\@(.*)"),0), recipient_domain = arrayindex(regextract(recipient_address,".*\@(.*)"),0 )
| filter sender_domain != $company_domain and recipient_domain contains $company_domain
| fields _time , sender_domain , recipient_domain , sender , recipients ,return_path  , *

data.name: Where the Emails by $sender Originated From
data.description: Lists each country the given emails from the given sender ($sender) originated from
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| alter country = sender_ip_location -> country,sender_address = sender -> address
| filter sender_address contains $sender
| fields country , sender_address , sender_ip , sender_ip_location ,*

data.name: Top Email Senders in the Organization
data.description: Lists the top emails senders in the organizaiton
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| alter sender_address = sender -> address
| alter recipients_address = arraymap(recipients , json_extract_scalar("@element","$.address"))
| arrayexpand recipients_address
| top sender_address

data.name: Top Email Recipients in the Organization
data.description: LIsts the top email recipients in the organizaiton
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| alter sender_address = sender -> address
| alter recipients_address = arraymap(recipients , json_extract_scalar("@element","$.address"))
| arrayexpand recipients_address
| top recipients_address

data.name: Emails with SPF/DKIM Verification Failure
data.description: Lists the emails that failed their DKIM/SPF verification
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| filter to_string(authentication_results) contains "*"
| fields _time , sender , recipients , authentication_results, dkim_signature , received_spf , internet_message_headers , *

data.name: Email Correspondance by $conversationid
data.description: Lists the entire email correspondance by the email conversation ID ($conversationid)
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| filter conversation_id contains $conversationid
| fields conversation_id , conversation_index , sender , recipients , internet_message_headers , o365_data , *

data.name: BCC Emails Sent by Sender ($sender) and Recipients
data.description: Lists the emails that were sent as BCC (Blind Carbon Copy) to additional recipients without the primary recipient being made aware. This query includes the sender and BCC recipients.
data.query_text:
dataset = email_data
| alter country = sender_ip_location -> country,sender_address = sender -> address
| filter sender_address contains $sender
| filter bcc_recipients  != null
| fields bcc_recipients , sender ,sender_address ,*

data.name: AWS | IAM Policy Changes Allowing Access to Resources
data.description: Details the events with a recent policy created that allows accessing any resource
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "CreatePolicyVersion"
| alter policyDocument = json_extract_scalar(requestParameters, "$.policyDocument")
| filter policyDocument != null
| alter statement = json_extract_array(policyDocument , "$.Statement")
| arrayexpand statement
| alter Effect = statement -> Effect , Resource = json_extract_array(statement , "$.Resource")
| filter Effect = "Allow"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity , statement ,  sourceIPAddress ,  eventID   , userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, statement , *

data.name: AWS | IAM Policies Attached to User/Group/Role
data.description: Lists the IAM Policies that were attached to a user/group/role
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName ~= "Attach\w+Policy$"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,  eventID   , userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Users Creating Access Keys for Different Users
data.description: Lists the AWS users who created a new ASW secret acces key and corresponding AWS access key ID for another specified user
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "CreateAccessKey"
| alter userName = userIdentity -> userName,targetUserName = requestParameters -> userName
| filter userName != targetUserName
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Users Creating Login Profiles for Different Users
data.description: Lists the events of users who created login passwords for another user
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "CreateLoginProfile"
| alter userIdentity_arn = userIdentity -> arn,targetUserName = requestParameters -> userName
| filter userIdentity_arn not contains targetUserName
| fields userIdentity_arn, targetUserName , requestID, requestParameters, recipientAccountId, responseElements , *

data.name: AWS | Attempts to Retrieve Admin Password for Running Windows EC2 Instance
data.description: Lists the events of Credential Access attempts to a EC2 windows instance
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName="GetPasswordData" and eventSource = "ec2.amazonaws.com"
| alter instanceId = requestParameters -> instanceId, principalId = userIdentity -> principalId
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Changes to RDS Master User Password
data.description: Lists the events for the attempts to reset the master user password of the RDS resource
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "ModifyDBInstance"
| alter masterUserPassword= json_extract_scalar(requestParameters, "$.masterUserPassword")
| filter masterUserPassword!= null
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Attempts to Delete Log Groups in AWS CloudWatch
data.description: Lists the events where users deleted the Amazon CloudWatch Log Group and deleted the archived logs
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "DeleteLogGroup"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Attempts to Delete or Stop Log Trails in AWS CloudTrail
data.description: Lists the events where users deleted or stopped the Amazon CloudTrail trail
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName in ("DeleteTrail","StopLogging") and eventSource = "cloudtrail.amazonaws.com"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Attempts to Tamper with AWS Security Measures
data.description: Lists the events where users deleted monitoring or security components in AWS
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName  in ("DeleteLogStream","DeleteDetector","DeleteIPSet","DeleteWebACL","DeleteRule","DeleteRuleGroup","DeleteLoggingConfiguration")
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Attempts to List Image Scan Findings in AWS
data.description: Lists the events where users were trying to list vulnerability findings of existing images within the ECR registry
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventSource="ecr.amazonaws.com" and eventName="DescribeImageScanFindings"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Exposed Security Groups for Sensitive Management Ports
data.description: Lists the events where AWS Security Groups where changed to accept inbound traffic to the internet, and filters for managment ports (RDP, SSH)
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress"
| alter items = json_extract_array(requestParameters,"$.ipPermissions.items")
| arrayexpand items
| alter toPort = items->toPort
| filter toPort in ("3389","22") and requestParameters contains "0.0.0.0/0"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , toPort,items, userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Console Login Failures by the Root Account Users
data.description: Lists the events where the AWS Root Account Users failed to login to the AWS console
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "ConsoleLogin" | alter userType = json_extract_scalar(userIdentity , "$.type") , outcome = json_extract_scalar(responseElements ,"$.ConsoleLogin"), MFAUsed = json_extract_scalar(additionalEventData,"$.MFAUsed")
| filter userType = "Root" and outcome = "Failure" and MFAUsed = "No"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName, userIdentity, userType, outcome , MFAUsed, sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | RDS Instances Made Publicly Accessible
data.description: Lists the events where the changes in RDS made them publicly accessible from the internet
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "ModifyDBInstance"
| alter publiclyAccessible = json_extract_scalar(requestParameters, "$.publiclyAccessible")
| filter publiclyAccessible = "true"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | MFA Devices Removed or Disabled
data.description: Lists events where the AWS Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) device was removed, and as a result the MFA was disabled.
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName= "DeleteVirtualMFADevice" OR eventName="DeactivateMFADevice"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | S3 Bucket ACLs Settings Changed to Publicly Exposed or Internet Facing
data.description: Lists the events where changes to the Amazon S3 bucket ACL caused them to be publicly accessible
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "PutBucketPublicAccessBlock"
| alter PublicAccessBlockConfiguration = json_extract(requestParameters , "$.PublicAccessBlockConfiguration")
| filter PublicAccessBlockConfiguration contains "true"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Multiple Failed Logins by the Same IP
data.description: Lists the events with multiple failed login attempts by the same IP events for AWS
data.query_text:
dataset =  amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "ConsoleLogin"
| alter outcome = responseElements -> ConsoleLogin , arn = userIdentity -> arn
| filter outcome = "Failure"
| comp count() as c, values(arn), values(userAgent) by sourceIPAddress, outcome, eventName
| filter c > 10

data.name: AWS | S3 Bucket ACLs with Disabled Block Public Access
data.description: Lists the events where the AWS S3 Block Public Access was disabled for the Amazon S3 bucket, which can indicate a denial of service attack
data.query_text:
dataset =  amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "BucketBlockPublicAccessDisabled"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | S3 Server Access Logging Disabled
data.description:
Lists the events where the AWS S3 server access logging setting was disabled for a bucket.
Server access logging provides detailed records for the requests that are made to a bucket.
data.query_text:
dataset =  amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "PutBucketLogging" and requestParameters not contains "LoggingEnabled"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Cloudwatch Alarms Deleted
data.description: Lists the events where the AWS CloudWatch alarms have been deleted
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "DeleteAlarms" and eventSource = "monitoring.amazonaws.com"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , *

data.name: AWS | EC2 Packet Capturing created
data.description: Lists the events where the AWS EC2 Network Packet Capture setting was added
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName contains "CreateTrafficMirror"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Requests to Recover Passwords
data.description: Lists the events of IAM users who requested to recover their AWS console access password
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "PasswordRecoveryRequested"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName, userIdentity,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Adding IAM Users to Admin Group
data.description: Lists the events where IAM users were added to the Admin Groups in AWS
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "AddUserToGroup"
| alter groupName = json_extract_scalar(requestParameters, "$.groupName")
| filter groupName ~= "A|admin"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName, userIdentity,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , groupName , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Events where S3 Bucket Versioning Disabled
data.description: Lists the events where the settings in the S3 buckets were changed so that the bucket object versioning was disabled
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName contains "PutBucketVersioning"
| alter Status = json_extract_scalar(requestParameters, "$.VersioningConfiguration.Status")
| filter Status = "Suspended"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName, userIdentity,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters ,Status, responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: AWS | Accounts where EBS Encryption By Default Disabled
data.description: Lists the events, where EBS encryption by default to a specifiec account, was disabled
data.query_text:
dataset = amazon_aws_raw
| filter eventName = "DisableEbsEncryptionByDefault"
| fields _time , recipientAccountId, eventName , userIdentity ,  sourceIPAddress ,eventID, userAgent , eventSource , requestParameters , responseElements , errorCode, *

data.name: Vulnerabilities Associated with a Given Endpoint ($endpoint)
data.description: Lists all the related vulnerabilities associated to a specific endpoint for the XDR agents installed
data.query_text:
dataset = va_endpoints
| filter endpoint_name contains  $endpoint

data.name: Endpoints with High or Critical Risk Vulnerabilities
data.description: Lists all the endpoint vulnerabilities that have a high or critcal risk severity for the XDR agents installed
data.query_text:
dataset = va_endpoints
| filter severity in(ENUM.CRITICAL , ENUM.HIGH )

data.name: Vulnerabilities Affecting More Than One Endpoint
data.description: Lists the vulnerabilities that affect multiple endpoints for the XDR agents installed
data.query_text:
dataset = va_cves
| filter array_length(affected_hosts ) > 1
| fields affected_hosts, affected_hosts_count, affected_products, name, description, *

data.name: Vulnerabilities Relating to Android and Linux Operating Systems
data.description: Lists the vulnerabilities that were found on an Android or Linux operating system for the vulnerability type called "APPLICATION_AND_OS"
data.query_text:
dataset = va_cves
| filter os_type in (ENUM.LINUX, ENUM.ANDROID) and (type = ENUM.APPLICATION_AND_OS)
| fields affected_hosts, affected_hosts_count, affected_products, name, description, *

data.name: CVEs/Vulnerabilities with High Impact and Exploitability Scores
data.description: Lists all the CVEs and vulnerabilities with a high impact and exploitability scores, where each score is greater than 5, for the XDR agents installed
data.query_text:
dataset = va_cves
| filter impact_score > 5 and exploitability_score > 5
| fields affected_hosts, affected_hosts_count, affected_products, name, description, *

data.name: Software Components Causing Most Vulnerability Findings
data.description: Calculates the software components or applications with vulnerabilities for the XDR agents installed
data.query_text:
dataset = va_cves
| arrayexpand affected_products
| filter type = ENUM.APPLICATION
| comp count() as c , values(name) as name, sum(affected_hosts_count ) as affected_hosts_count , values(severity_score) as severity_score, values(description) as description by affected_products
| fields affected_products , description , name , affected_hosts_count , c , severity_score
| sort desc c

data.name: Vulnerability Findings for Specifiec CVE
data.description: Lists the vulnerability findings for a specifiec CVE or CVE ID according to the XDR agents installed
data.query_text:
dataset = va_cves
| filter name contains $cve or cve_id contains $cve
| fields name, cve_id , affected_products , affected_hosts , severity , description, *

data.name: Critical Operating System Vulnerabilities
data.description: Lists the critical Operating System vulnerabilities for the XDR agents installed
data.query_text:
dataset = va_cves
| filter type = ENUM.OPERATING_SYSTEM and severity = ENUM.CRITICAL
| fields name, cve_id , affected_products , affected_hosts , severity , description, *

data.name: Office 365 | Unread Emails with Attachments Older Than 48 Hours in the Last 60 days
data.description: Lists the unread emails with attachments in Microsoft O365 that are older than 48 hours in the last 7 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = msft_o365_emails_raw
| alter email_create_time = createdDateTime
| alter currenttime = current_time()
| alter time_diff_in_hours = timestamp_diff (currenttime, email_create_time, "HOUR")
| filter hasAttachments = true and isRead = false and time_diff_in_hours > 48
| fields email_create_time, currenttime, time_diff_in_hours, *

data.name: Office 365 | Emails with Attachments and Excessive CC Recipients in the Last 30 Days
data.description: Lists the emails with attachments in Microsoft O365 that were sent to more than 5 CC recipients, excluding the PANW domain, in the last 30 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 30d
| dataset = msft_o365_emails_raw
| alter sender_mail = json_extract(sender, "$.emailAddress.address")
| alter sender_domain = regextract(sender_mail, "@([^\"]+)")
| alter cc = ccRecipients -> []
| alter cc_recipients_len = array_length(cc)
| filter hasAttachments = true and cc_recipients_len > 5 and sender_domain not contains "@paloaltonetworks"
| fields sender, sender_domain, ccRecipients, cc, cc_recipients_len, *

data.name: Count SaaS Products Accessed in the Last 7 Days
data.description: Counts the number of distinct SaaS products accessed in the Last 7 Days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = saas_audit_logs
| comp count_distinct(product) by product
| fields product, *

data.name: SaaS Operations Performed in the Last 7 Days
data.description: Lists the number of distinct operations in SaaS environments performed by the name of the operation in the Last 7 Days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 7d
| dataset = saas_audit_logs
| comp count_distinct(operation_name) by operation_name
| fields operation_name, *

data.name: SaaS Successful Privilege Grants Operations in the Last 10 Days
data.description: Lists the successful privilege grant operations in SaaS environments performed in the last 10 days
data.query_text:
config timeframe = 10d
| dataset = saas_audit_logs
| filter operation_name = "PRIVILEGE_GRANT"
| filter operation_status = "SUCCESS"
| alter user = identity_normalized -> username
| fields user, operation_name_orig, *

data.name: Authentication Story Sources
data.description: Lists the sources (associated_products) for the authentication_story preset
data.query_text:
preset = authentication_story
| comp values(associated_products) as sources
| arrayexpand sources

data.name: Authentication Story Top 10 Sources
data.description: Identifies the top 10 sources for the authentication_story preset
data.query_text:
preset = authentication_story
| comp count() as event_count by associated_products
| sort desc event_count
| limit 10

data.name: Authentication Story Top 10 Failed Clients
data.description: Lists the top 10 clients that failed to authenticate in the authentication_story preset
data.query_text:
preset = authentication_story
| filter auth_outcome = "FAILURE"
| comp count(auth_client) as failed_count by auth_client
| sort desc failed_count
| limit 10

data.name: Authentication Story Top 10 Authentication Failed Reasons
data.description: Lists the top 10 reasons for failing to authenticate in the authentication_story preset
data.query_text:
preset = authentication_story
| filter auth_outcome = "FAILURE"
| comp count(auth_outcome_reason) as failed_count by auth_outcome_reason
| sort desc failed_count
| limit 10

data.name: Potential NetWire RAT Activity
data.description: Detects registry keys related to NetWire RAT.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4 and event_sub_type = 1
 | filter (action_registry_key_name contains "\software\NetWire")
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name

data.name: Potential Persistence Via New AMSI Providers
data.description: Detects when an attacker registers a new AMSI provider in order to achieve persistence.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4 and event_sub_type = 1
 | filter (action_registry_key_name contains "\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\AMSI\Providers" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\AMSI\Providers") and not (actor_process_image_path ~= "C:\\Windows\\System32\\.*" OR actor_process_image_path ~= "C:\\Program Files\\.*" OR actor_process_image_path ~= "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\.*")
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name

data.name: Potential COM Object Hijacking Via TreatAs Subkey
data.description: Detects COM object hijacking via TreatAs subkey.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4 and event_sub_type = 1
 | filter (action_registry_key_name contains "HKU" AND action_registry_key_name contains "Classes\CLSID" AND action_registry_key_name contains "\TreatAs") and not (((actor_process_image_path IN ("C:\WINDOWS\system32\svchost.exe"))))
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name

data.name: Potential Persistence Via Disk Cleanup Handler

action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\BranchCache" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Content Indexer Cleaner" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\D3D Shader Cache" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Delivery Optimization Files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Device Driver Packages" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Diagnostic Data Viewer database files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Downloaded Program Files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\DownloadsFolder" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Feedback Hub Archive log files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Internet Cache Files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Language Pack" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Microsoft Office Temp Files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Offline Pages Files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Old ChkDsk Files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Previous Installations" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Recycle Bin" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\RetailDemo Offline Content" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Setup Log Files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\System error memory dump files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\System error minidump files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Temporary Files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Temporary Setup Files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Temporary Sync Files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Thumbnail Cache" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Update Cleanup" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Upgrade Discarded Files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\User file versions" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Windows Defender" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Windows Error Reporting Files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Windows ESD installation files" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Windows Upgrade Log Files")
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name

data.name: PUA - Sysinternal Tool Execution

 | filter event_type = 4 and event_sub_type = 1

data.name: Suspicious Execution Of Renamed Sysinternals Tools
data.description: Detects the creation of the accepteula key related to the Sysinternals tools being created from executables with the wrong name (e.g. a renamed Sysinternals tool).
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4 and event_sub_type = 1
 | filter (action_registry_key_name contains "\Active Directory Explorer" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\Handle" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\LiveKd" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\ProcDump" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\Process Explorer" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\PsExec" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\PsLoggedon" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\PsLoglist" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\PsPasswd" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\PsPing" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\PsService" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\SDelete") AND (action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\EulaAccepted") and not (actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\ADExplorer.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\ADExplorer64.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\handle.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\handle64.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\livekd.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\livekd64.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\procdump.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\procdump64.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\procexp.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\procexp64.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\PsExec.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\PsExec64.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\PsLoggedon.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\PsLoggedon64.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\psloglist.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\psloglist64.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\pspasswd.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\pspasswd64.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\PsPing.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\PsPing64.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\PsService.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\PsService64.exe" OR actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\sdelete.exe")
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name

data.name: PUA - Sysinternals Tools Execution
data.description: Detects the execution of some potentially unwanted tools such as PsExec, Procdump, etc. (part of the Sysinternals suite) via the creation of the accepteula registry key.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4 and event_sub_type = 1
 | filter (action_registry_key_name contains "\Active Directory Explorer" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\Handle" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\LiveKd" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\Process Explorer" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\ProcDump" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\PsExec" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\PsLoglist" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\PsPasswd" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\SDelete" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\Sysinternals") AND (action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\EulaAccepted")
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name

data.name: Removal of Potential COM Hijacking Registry Keys
data.description: Detects any deletion of entries in .*\shell\open\command registry keys.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4 and event_sub_type = 2
 | filter (action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\shell\\open\\command") and not (((actor_process_image_path IN ("C:\Windows\system32\svchost.exe"))) OR ((actor_process_image_path ~= "C:\\Program Files\\Common Files\\Microsoft Shared\\ClickToRun\\.*" OR actor_process_image_path ~= "C:\\Program Files\\Common Files\\Microsoft Shared\\ClickToRun\\Updates\\.*") AND (actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\OfficeClickToRun.exe")) OR ((actor_process_image_path IN ("C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Office\root\integration\integrator.exe"))) OR ((actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\Dropbox.exe") AND (action_registry_key_name contains "\Dropbox.")) OR ((actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\Wireshark_uninstaller.exe") AND (action_registry_key_name contains "\wireshark-capture-file")) OR ((actor_process_image_path ~= "C:\\Program Files\\Opera\\.*" OR actor_process_image_path ~= "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Opera\\.*") AND (actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\installer.exe")) OR ((actor_process_image_path contains "peazip") AND (action_registry_key_name contains "\PeaZip.")) OR ((actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\Everything.exe") AND (action_registry_key_name contains "\Everything.")) OR ((actor_process_image_path ~= "C:\\Windows\\Installer\\MSI.*")) OR ((actor_process_image_path ~= "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Java\\.*") AND (actor_process_image_path ~= ".*\\installer.exe") AND (action_registry_key_name contains "\Classes\WOW6432Node\CLSID\{4299124F-F2C3-41b4-9C73-9236B2AD0E8F}")))
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name

 | filter event_type = 4
 | filter (action_registry_key_name ~= ".*Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\Backup Mgr")
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name, action_registry_value_name

data.name: Run Once Task Configuration in Registry
data.description: Rule to detect the configuration of Run Once registry key.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4
 | filter (action_registry_key_name contains "\Microsoft\Active Setup\Installed Components") AND (action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\StubPath") and not (((action_registry_value_name contains "C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application" AND action_registry_value_name contains "\Installer\chrmstp.exe\" --configure-user-settings --verbose-logging --system-level")) OR ((action_registry_value_name contains "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft\Edge\Application" OR action_registry_value_name contains "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\Edge\Application") AND (action_registry_value_name ~= ".*\\Installer\\setup.exe\\" --configure-user-settings --verbose-logging --system-level --msedge --channel=stable")))
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name, action_registry_value_name

data.name: Shell Open Registry Keys Manipulation
data.description: Detects the shell open key manipulation (exefile and ms-settings) used for persistence and the pattern of UAC Bypass using fodhelper.exe, computerdefaults.exe, slui.exe via registry keys (e.g. UACMe 33 or 62).
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4
 | filter (action_registry_key_name ~= ".*Classes\\ms-settings\\shell\\open\\command\\SymbolicLinkValue") AND (action_registry_value_name contains "\Software\Classes\{") or (action_registry_key_name ~= ".*Classes\\ms-settings\\shell\\open\\command\\DelegateExecute") or ((action_registry_key_name ~= ".*Classes\\ms-settings\\shell\\open\\command\\(Default)" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*Classes\\exefile\\shell\\open\\command\\(Default)") and not (action_registry_value_name IN ("(Empty)")))
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name, action_registry_value_name

data.name: Potential Credential Dumping Via LSASS SilentProcessExt Technique
data.description: Detects changes to the Registry in which a monitor program gets registered to dump the memory of the lsass.exe process.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4
 | filter (action_registry_key_name contains "Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\SilentProcessExit\lsass.exe")
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name, action_registry_value_name

data.name: Security Support Provider (SSP) Added to LSA Configuration
data.description: Detects the addition of a SSP to the registry.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4
 | filter (action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Control\\Lsa\\Security Packages" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\Control\\Lsa\\OSConfig\\Security Packages") and not (((actor_process_image_path IN ("C:\Windows\system32\msiexec.exe", "C:\Windows\syswow64\MsiExec.exe"))))
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name, action_registry_value_name

data.name: Sticky Key Like Backdoor Usage
data.description: Detects the usage and installation of a backdoor that uses an option to register a malicious debugger for built-in tools that are accessible in the login screen.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4
 | filter (action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\sethc.exe\\Debugger" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\utilman.exe\\Debugger" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\osk.exe\\Debugger" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\Magnify.exe\\Debugger" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\Narrator.exe\\Debugger" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\DisplaySwitch.exe\\Debugger" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\atbroker.exe\\Debugger" OR action_registry_key_name ~= ".*\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\HelpPane.exe\\Debugger")
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name, action_registry_value_name

data.name: Atbroker Registry Change
data.description: Detects creation/modification of Assistive Technology applications and persistence with usage of 'at'.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4
 | filter (action_registry_key_name contains "Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Accessibility\ATs" OR action_registry_key_name contains "Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Accessibility\Configuration") and not (((actor_process_image_path IN ("C:\Windows\system32\atbroker.exe")) AND (action_registry_key_name contains "\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Accessibility\Configuration") AND (action_registry_value_name IN ("(Empty)"))) OR ((actor_process_image_path ~= "C:\\Windows\\Installer\\MSI.*") AND (action_registry_key_name contains "Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Accessibility\ATs")))
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name, action_registry_value_name

data.name: Suspicious Run Key from Download
data.description: Detects the suspicious RUN keys created by software located in Download or temporary Outlook/Internet Explorer directories.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4
 | filter (actor_process_image_path contains "\Downloads" OR actor_process_image_path contains "\Temporary Internet Files\Content.Outlook" OR actor_process_image_path contains "\Local Settings\Temporary Internet Files") AND (action_registry_key_name contains "\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run")
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name, action_registry_value_name

data.name: DLL Load via LSASS
data.description: Detects a method to load DLL via LSASS process using an undocumented Registry key.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4
 | filter (action_registry_key_name contains "\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\DirectoryServiceExtPt" OR action_registry_key_name contains "\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\LsaDbExtPt") and not (((actor_process_image_path IN ("C:\Windows\system32\lsass.exe")) AND (action_registry_value_name IN ("%%systemroot%%\system32\ntdsa.dll", "%%systemroot%%\system32\lsadb.dll"))))
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name, action_registry_value_name

data.name: Suspicious Camera and Microphone Access
data.description: Detects Processes accessing the camera and microphone from suspicious folder.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4
 | filter (((action_registry_key_name contains "\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\CapabilityAccessManager\ConsentStore" AND action_registry_key_name contains "\NonPackaged")) AND ((action_registry_key_name contains "microphone" OR action_registry_key_name contains "webcam")) AND ((action_registry_key_name contains ":#Windows#Temp#" OR action_registry_key_name contains ":#$Recycle.bin#" OR action_registry_key_name contains ":#Temp#" OR action_registry_key_name contains ":#Users#Public#" OR action_registry_key_name contains ":#Users#Default#" OR action_registry_key_name contains ":#Users#Desktop#")))
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name, action_registry_value_name

data.name: Enable Remote Connection Between Anonymous Computer - AllowAnonymousCallback
data.description: Detects enabling of the AllowAnonymousCallback registry value, which allows a remote connection between computers that do not have a trust relationship.
data.query_text:
dataset = xdr_data
 | filter event_type = 4
 | filter (action_registry_key_name contains "\Microsoft\WBEM\CIMOM\AllowAnonymousCallback") AND (action_registry_value_name IN ("DWORD (0x00000001)"))
 | fields agent_hostname, event_type, event_sub_type, actor_process_image_path, actor_process_os_pid, actor_process_image_md5, actor_process_signature_vendor, actor_process_signature_status, actor_effective_username, action_registry_key_name, action_registry_value_name

data.name: Network Connection Initiated By AddinUtil.EXE

dataset = xdr_data
